pred_reason: The SWC-101 label applies due to the identified integer underflow vulnerability in 'uint256 id = 27 - tokenId' where tokenId > 27 causes critical underflow. The SWC-107 label is justified because of the hardcoded address restriction found in the onERC721Received function, which creates a trust assumption in external contracts. Evidence: Critical underflow vulnerability in 'uint256 id = 27 - tokenId' calculation (Finding 4); Hardcoded address restriction in onERC721Received function (Finding 11); State changes occurring after external calls in onERC721Received (Finding 2)

raw.pred_reason: The SWC-101 label applies due to the identified integer underflow vulnerability in 'uint256 id = 27 - tokenId' where tokenId > 27 causes critical underflow. The SWC-107 label is justified because of the hardcoded address restriction found in the onERC721Received function, which creates a trust assumption in external contracts. Evidence: Critical underflow vulnerability in 'uint256 id = 27 - tokenId' calculation (Finding 4); Hardcoded address restriction in onERC721Received function (Finding 11); State changes occurring after external calls in onERC721Received (Finding 2)

The SWC-107 label is justified because of the hardcoded address restriction found in the onERC721Received function, which creates a trust assumption in external contracts.
evidence: Critical underflow vulnerability in 'uint256 id = 27 - tokenId' calculation (Finding 4)
